task_completion.description: The document includes a Title Page with the correct Document ID.

task_completion.description: The document includes a Table of Contents and page numbers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a generated Table of Contents section and page numbers visible in the header or footer of the document pages.

task_completion.description: The document contains a table for Functional Requirements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a table in the document listing functional requirements, with IDs like REQ-F-001 to REQ-F-012.

task_completion.description: The document contains a table for Use Cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a table listing use cases, with IDs like UC-001 to UC-004.

task_completion.description: The document contains a table for External Interfaces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a table listing external interfaces, with IDs like IF-001 to IF-003.

task_completion.description: The document contains a table for the Data Dictionary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a table listing data entities such as Patient, Provider, Appointment, Location, InsurancePlan, etc.

task_completion.description: The document is saved with the correct filename in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the OpenOffice Writer window title bar or a file manager window showing 'SRS_Appointment_Module_v1.odt' saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used Apache OpenOffice Writer to create the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Apache OpenOffice Writer GUI being actively used to edit and format the document.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script or command-line tool to generate the ODT file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the terminal windows to ensure the agent didn't run a script (e.g., Python, pandoc) to bypass manual creation in OpenOffice.

integrity.description: The tables in the document are native OpenOffice tables.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for table gridlines and table formatting options being used in OpenOffice, rather than plain text formatted with spaces or tabs to look like a table.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The JSON source contains 12 functional requirements (REQ-F-001 to REQ-F-012), 4 use cases (UC-001 to UC-004), 3 external interfaces (IF-001 to IF-003), and 8 data dictionary entities (Patient, Provider, Appointment, Location, InsurancePlan, WaitlistEntry, RecurringSchedule, AuditRecord). The document must include these structured into tables. The Document ID is PHS-SRS-APT-2024-001. The output file must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/SRS_Appointment_Module_v1.odt.
